hero.title: A better way to manage procurement across automotive operations
hero.description: TalepNET helps automotive companies manage procurement across plants, branches, warehouses, service operations, and central teams with connected request, approval, sourcing, supplier, order, receiving, and spend workflows.
highlights: Connect plant demand, approvals, and execution in one workflow
Bring supplier, quotation, and order history closer to decisions
Keep contracts and contracted suppliers visible during sourcing
Track receiving and supplier follow-through after order placement
Use spend visibility to support control across distributed operations

automotive.intro: Automotive procurement is not just about buying parts. It is about keeping maintenance, production support, service operations, and indirect spend moving without losing approval discipline or delivery visibility.

problem.eyebrow: Why automotive procurement becomes hard to control
problem.title: Distributed operations create workflow breaks quickly
problem.description: Plants, branches, depots, and service teams often buy under different conditions. When requests, approvals, supplier communication, and receiving are spread across email, calls, and spreadsheets, procurement loses consistency and management loses timing.
points: Different sites follow different purchasing habits
Urgent demand bypasses approval discipline
Supplier communication becomes fragmented
Contracted suppliers are not always visible at purchase time
Orders and receipts become harder to follow centrally

fit.title: Central control and site execution can live in the same system
fit.description: TalepNET supports multi-role, multi-branch procurement with configurable workflows, purchasing execution, supplier collaboration, receiving, and spend visibility in one connected operating model.

capabilities.title: One operating model across demand, sourcing, execution, and spend control

items.title: Structured demand capture across locations
items.text: Standardize requests for maintenance teams, plant users, service units, and central buyers through controlled forms and request flows.

items.title: Approval workflows shaped by real authority structures
items.text: Reflect departments, thresholds, and roles in approval routing so control follows how decisions are actually made.

items.title: Better sourcing and supplier comparison
items.text: Convert approved demand into purchase forms, compare quotations, reuse previous order history, and surface contracted suppliers before committing spend.

items.title: Connected order and receiving execution
items.text: Keep purchase orders, supplier communication, delivery context, and receiving tied to the same purchasing chain.

useCases.eyebrow: Built for real automotive procurement scenarios
useCases.title: Flexible enough for plants, service operations, and central purchasing

items.title: Plant and branch purchasing
items.text: Standardize local demand capture while preserving workflow flexibility across plants, service centers, depots, and administrative units.

items.title: MRO and operational supply buying
items.text: Manage recurring purchases for maintenance, equipment support, consumables, and day-to-day operational continuity.

items.title: Supplier-heavy sourcing environments
items.text: Improve quote comparison, supplier response handling, and contracted supplier reuse where multiple vendors compete in recurring categories.

items.title: Multi-location spend control
items.text: Bring visibility across branches and cost centers so procurement teams can see where spend is accumulating and where discipline is weakening.

flow.title: Keep requests, supplier decisions, and receiving connected across the same process.
flow.description: Requests, approvals, purchasing, supplier decisions, orders, and receiving remain part of one visible chain instead of separate operational handoffs.
steps: Teams submit requests with cost center and operational context.
Approvals follow the configured organizational logic.
Purchasing converts approved demand into purchase forms.
Buyers collect quotations, compare suppliers, and reuse contract intelligence.
Orders are placed through structured supplier-facing workflows.
Receiving records what actually arrived and closes the loop.

benefits.eyebrow: What automotive teams gain with TalepNET
benefits.title: Better coordination across distributed operations without losing control

items.title: Better control across distributed operations
items.text: Plants and branches move through one connected model without losing local execution speed.

items.title: Better supplier coordination
items.text: RFQ, quote, order, and receiving activity stay attached to the process instead of being scattered.

items.title: Better commercial discipline
items.text: Contracted suppliers, previous orders, and quotation comparison give buyers stronger decision support.

items.title: Better financial control
items.text: Cost center, budget, and spend visibility help teams control procurement while operations are still moving.

finalCta.eyebrow: Bring automotive procurement into one connected model
finalCta.title: Connect plant demand, approvals, sourcing, suppliers, orders, and receiving in one operational system.
finalCta.description: TalepNET gives automotive organizations a clearer way to manage supplier coordination and spend visibility across distributed operations.

hero.title: A better way to manage purchasing across construction projects and sites
hero.description: TalepNET helps construction companies manage requests, approvals, suppliers, sourcing, purchase orders, receiving, and spend visibility in one connected workflow.
highlights: Standardize project and site demand capture
Keep approvals aligned with real project authority
Improve supplier coordination across active work
Move cleanly from sourcing into execution
Keep cost pressure visible while projects stay active

construction.intro: Construction procurement is shaped by site urgency, supplier dependency, budget pressure, and multi-location execution. TalepNET gives teams a more structured way to control that complexity without slowing the field down.

problem.eyebrow: Why construction procurement is hard to keep under control?
problem.title: Site pressure and disconnected purchasing practices make consistency harder to maintain
problem.description: Construction organizations often buy under pressure. When demand starts on calls, WhatsApp messages, or spreadsheets, procurement loses consistency and finance loses timing.
points: Project and site demand arrives through fragmented channels
Urgent purchasing bypasses approval and budget discipline
Supplier communication is hard to track across RFQs and orders
Different locations follow different procurement habits
Spend becomes visible too late

fit.title: Operational flexibility and procurement structure can coexist
fit.description: TalepNET is built for organizations that need operational flexibility without sacrificing workflow discipline, supplier coordination, or spend visibility.

capabilities.title: One process chain across sites, suppliers, execution, and cost control

items.title: Centralize project and site demand

items.text: Reflect departments, thresholds, and operational structures in routing logic without forcing every site into the same manual routine.

items.title: Source faster without losing commercial discipline
items.text: Generate purchase forms from approved demand, collect quotations, and reuse supplier history before making a decision.

items.title: Manage receipt and execution at project level
items.text: Record actual delivered quantities and keep order follow-through visible after supplier selection.

nonProfit.intro: Nonprofits need to know not only what has been spent, but what a new request or purchase will mean for the budget structure behind it. TalepNET helps bring that visibility closer to the purchasing process itself.

problem.eyebrow: Why nonprofit procurement becomes harder to review over time
problem.title: Mission-driven organizations still need structured financial control
problem.description: When requests, approvals, suppliers, and receipts sit in separate routines, nonprofits lose the continuity they need for accountability, budget control, and operational review.
points: Demand can start in fragmented channels
Budget ownership is not always visible early enough
Workflow history becomes harder to reconstruct later
Supplier decisions are separated from the original request
Execution and receiving may sit outside the record

fit.eyebrow: Keep a clearer process history and cleaner records
fit.title: Audit-ready visibility without rebuilding the history later
fit.description: TalepNET keeps requests, approvals, supplier decisions, purchase actions, order steps, and receiving activity tied to the same operational record.
